title: Cube Sum PASS: extended witnesses — non-cube sums confirm uniqueness
_fixture_purpose: Extended witness set showing that (2,1) is the unique QA direction for which F³+C³+G³ is a perfect cube. Other primitive QA directions have non-cube sums: (3,2)→4050, (4,1)→8800, (4,3)→29792. This empirically confirms uniqueness in the QA direction space. The fundamental (2,1) is singled out because F³+C³+G³=216=6³ requires the simultaneous Pythagorean structure (3²+4²=5²) and the cube sum structure (3³+4³+5³=6³) — a dual coincidence that only occurs at the fundamental.

witnesses.check: 3³+4³+5³=216=6³ ✓ — the unique QA direction with perfect cube sum
5³+12³+13³=125+1728+2197=4050; cube root ≈15.94; not a perfect cube
15³+8³+17³=3375+512+4913=8800; cube root ≈20.6; not a perfect cube
7³+24³+25³=343+13824+15625=29792; cube root ≈30.9; not a perfect cube

connections.cert_130: Origin of 24: same fundamental triple (3,4,5); H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=24 → minimum 24
connections.cert_137: Koenig twisted squares: (I²,2CF,G²,H²)=(1,24,25,49) for (2,1); 6=√(CF)? No; but 216=9×24
connections.cert_135: Pythagorean tree root (2,1); F³+C³+G³=216 is a cube-root-of-sum property of the tree root only
